raw_consumer_statement: Filed a dispute on a charge. I then cancelled the dispute as it appeared the issue was resolved with the merchant. Employee said if I cancel the dispute, I can not reopen it again. I told him my issues and that I only want to proceed IF I can open the dispute up again IF my issue does not get resolved with the merchant. The employee agreed and said that he would include that info in the notes. I later tried to reopen the dispute and was told that I cant. I explained the agreement that the employee made with me and reminded them that there would be a recording. They advised they would look into it. I called back and again inquired and was told the same thing. I spoke to a supervisor who said said that a recording does exist and that she would request that it be looked at again. I then received a letter saying that it is absolutely impossible to reopen a dispute after cancelling it. That is simply their policy. Their employee made a deal with me and the XXXX company is not honoring it because they have waited so long, that the merchant now no longer exists.
